verdict_reason: If 2.42 were removed and only the chapter thesis plus 2.41 (resolved buddhi) plus 2.43 (predicative content) plus 2.45 (operative imperative) remained, would the engineering analog be reconstructible? No. The 'puṣpitā vāc with implicit na anyad asti closure clause' framing requires the specific 2.42 grammatical structure (the relative-clause launch). 2.43 supplies what is in the speech; 2.45 says what to do; 2.42 names the closure-clause diagnostic that connects them. The 'standing-review polish depends on the slides it doesn't have' analog cannot be derived from 2.43 or 2.45 alone — it requires 2.42's specific naming of the speakers and their characteristic closure clause. PASS.
